content: Just as classroom lexical 'reflexion' practice teaches human learners to connect word forms to their semantic context and correct usage errors through repeated reflective review, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can be augmented with LLM multi-perspective self-reflection by borrowing this pedagogical structure to ground abstract self-correction in concrete, vocabulary-aligned knowledge checks. This creates a hybrid self-reflection mechanism that reduces vague, ungrounded self-correction by tying LLM reflection to explicit linguistic schema that the model already learns during pre-training.
